name: Commercial Sheds
description:
Commercial sheds are steel clear-span structures built for businesses that customers and trade visitors actually walk into — a showroom, a retail outlet, a service centre, or a working workshop shed — rather than a closed factory floor. SAMAN manufactures them as a finished, customer-facing premises: a standard unit runs 60 × 36 × 14 ft (2,160 sq ft) from ₹21,55,000, with the width, height, frontage, and internal layout configured to how your business trades. We fabricate at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and install across India.

Commercial Shed vs Industrial Shed: What's the Difference?

A commercial shed is designed around people coming in — display space, a finished frontage, customer access, and an appearance that represents the business. An industrial shed is designed around production output — maximum column-free floor for machinery, materials, and logistics, with appearance secondary. Same steel engineering; different priorities. If your space sells, services, or shows to customers, you want a commercial shed.

A commercial shed set up as a showroom — glazed shopfront and forecourt parking for walk-in customers.

The practical difference shows up in three places: the frontage (a commercial shed gets a glazed shopfront or display-grade roller shutters, not a plain industrial roller door), the finish (clean internal lining and branding-ready external cladding), and the layout (a mix of customer area and back-of-house, rather than one open production span). For the full production-grade structure and span economics, see SAMAN's industrial shed range.

How a Commercial Shed Is Built for Customer-Facing Use

Every SAMAN commercial steel shed is a portal-frame structure with a clear span — no internal columns interrupting the floor — so you decide how the space is divided. The standard 60 × 36 ft footprint gives 2,160 sq ft and a 14 ft eaves height, enough for a display floor plus a mezzanine floor for office or stockroom use without raising the roofline.

The frontage choice in detail — a glazed shopfront alongside a roller-shutter bay on a white commercial shed.

The customer-facing elements are what separate this from a plain shed. The front bay can take a glazed shopfront for a showroom, full-height roller shutters for a workshop or service bay, or a combination of both. External walls use colour-coated, branding-ready cladding that carries signage and paint cleanly. Inside, lining and lighting are specified to retail or workshop standard rather than bare industrial finish. Width, height, door positions, and frontage are all set to your site and trade before fabrication begins.

Commercial Shed Configurations by Business Type

The same clear-span structure adapts to four common commercial uses. This is the starting point most buyers work from before customising:

Configured as a workshop — wide roller-shutter bays give vehicle and equipment access for service work.

Business type Clear span & height Frontage Best layout for
Showroom 30–40 ft span, 14 ft+ Glazed shopfront Open display floor + mezzanine office
Retail outlet 24–36 ft span, 12–14 ft Glazed entry + shutter Front sales area, rear storage
Workshop 36–50 ft span, 14–16 ft Wide roller shutters Open work bays, vehicle/equipment access
Service centre 36–40 ft span, 14 ft Shutters + customer entry Work bays + customer waiting/desk zone

A showroom shed prioritises glazing and display height; a workshop prioritises door width and bay access; a service centre needs both a work zone and a customer-facing desk. Tell us the trade and we configure the span and frontage to match.

What Drives the Price of a Commercial Shed

The standard 60 × 36 × 14 ft commercial shed is ₹21,55,000 — about ₹1,000 per sq ft for 2,160 sq ft of finished, customer-ready space including structure, roofing, cladding, and erection.

A finished 60×36 ft commercial shed — the scale and finish behind the ₹21,55,000 standard configuration.

Four things move that number: span width (wider clear spans need heavier sections), eaves height (taller frontage for showroom display), frontage finish (glazed shopfronts cost more than plain shutters), and whether you add a mezzanine floor or upgraded retail-grade flooring. The quantity of openings and the level of internal lining also factor in. We quote the exact configuration rather than a per-square-foot estimate, so the price reflects the premises you're actually building.

Commercial, Steel, or Storage Shed — Choosing the Right One

As a retail outlet — glazed sales frontage at the front, roller-shutter stockroom access at the rear.

A commercial shed is the right call when the space is customer-facing. If your decision is really about the structure's material and longevity rather than its use — galvanised versus mild steel, profile and grade — start with steel sheds built around material specification. If the building is purely for keeping goods and equipment with no customer or trade-display role, a dedicated storage shed is the more economical choice. When you're unsure, tell us how the space will be used and we'll point you to the right product in the shed line.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are the types of sheds, and where does a commercial shed fit?
Sheds split broadly by use: industrial sheds for factories and warehouses, storage sheds for goods, garden sheds for homes, and commercial sheds for customer-facing premises like showrooms, retail outlets, and workshops. A commercial shed is distinguished by its frontage, finish, and customer-access layout rather than its raw structure.

What does a commercial shed cost in India?
SAMAN's standard 60 × 36 ft (2,160 sq ft) commercial shed is ₹21,55,000 — roughly ₹1,000 per sq ft — including structure, roofing, cladding, and erection. Smaller or larger configurations are priced to the exact span, height, and frontage you specify.

Can a commercial shed be used as a showroom or retail outlet?
Yes — that's a primary use. A glazed shopfront, display-grade height, retail-standard internal finish, and a mezzanine for office or stock turn the clear-span shell into a working showroom or retail outlet.

How long does it take to build and install a commercial shed?
A standard commercial shed is fabricated and erected in roughly 6 to 12 weeks from order confirmation, depending on size, frontage complexity, and your site readiness. You arrange the civil foundation; our crew handles structural erection.

Get a Project Quote

Get a project quote for your commercial shed — sized and configured to your trade.

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
short_description:
60×36 ft commercial shed for showrooms, retail & workshops — clear-span steel, custom frontage.
Finished, customer-facing premises with glazed shopfront or roller-shutter options and a mezzanine floor add-on. Configured to your trade, fabricated and installed across India.
Size
60×36×14 ft
Capacity
2,160 sq ft
Frame
Steel clear-span
Cladding
Colour-coated
